# InfraBench

> InfraBench is a benchmark suite for evaluating AI agents on realistic infrastructure management tasks across the full system stack and operational lifecycle, with fine-grained risk assessment.

Published at HotInfra '26 (Workshop on Hot Topics in System Infrastructure), co-located with ISCA '26, Raleigh, NC.

## Canonical links

- Website: https://infraben.ch/
- Paper (PDF): https://hotinfra.org/2026/papers/hotinfra26-final71.pdf
- HotInfra 2026 workshop: https://hotinfra.org/2026/
- GitHub: https://github.com/XuanmiaoG/InfraBench
- Contribute: https://github.com/XuanmiaoG/InfraBench/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
- Lead author (Google Scholar): https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=oD9j2NMAAAAJ&hl=en

## Paper

- Title: Beyond Pass/Fail: Evaluating Infrastructure Agents Across Layers, Lifecycle, and Risk
- Venue: HotInfra '26 @ ISCA '26
- Authors: Yuan Gao, Zeren Yang, Junnan Li, Shawn (Wanxiang) Zhong (University of Wisconsin–Madison); Ahmed Dajani, Mai Zheng (Iowa State University); Andrea Arpaci-Dusseau, Remzi Arpaci-Dusseau (University of Wisconsin–Madison)

## What InfraBench evaluates

- Twelve seed tasks spanning hardware (L1), local systems (L2), distributed systems (L3), and user applications (L4)
- Full operational lifecycle checks beyond pass/fail (durability, invariants, cleanup, safety)
- Preliminary results: mean scores across seven agent–model configurations range roughly 56%–91%; no agent fully saturates the suite

## Affiliations & support

- University of Wisconsin–Madison · Iowa State University
- Task interviews: CHTC, UW–Madison DoIT, ARA Wireless Living Lab
- Compute: CloudLab
- API credits: Google Gemini Academic Program

## For citation

Prefer the camera-ready PDF title and author list. Project site and Scholar profile of Yuan Gao link to the same work.
